draft.introduction: 1 Samuel 16 recounts the anointing of David by Samuel. God rejects the outwardly impressive older brothers and chooses the youngest, the shepherd—“for the Lord sees not as man sees; man looks at the outward appearance, but the Lord looks at the heart.” From the Septuagint.

translation.text: When they came, he looked on Eliab and thought, “Surely the Lord’s anointed is before him.” But the Lord said to Samuel, “Do not look at his appearance or the height of his stature, because I have rejected him. For the Lord sees not as man sees: man looks at the outward appearance, but the Lord looks at the heart.”
translation.original_text: καὶ ἐγενήθη ἐν τῷ εἰσελθεῖν αὐτοὺς καὶ εἶδεν τὸν Ελιαβ

translation.text: And he sent and brought him in. Now he was ruddy and had beautiful eyes and was handsome. And the Lord said, “Arise, anoint him, for this is he.” Then Samuel took the horn of oil and anointed him in the midst of his brothers. And the Spirit of the Lord rushed upon David from that day forward.

translator_notes.note: The principle that God “looks at the heart” (κύριος ὄψεται εἰς καρδίαν) subverts human criteria of worthiness. David’s anointing establishes the messianic lineage; the word Christos (Christ) is simply the Greek translation of “anointed one” (mashiach).
